channel.title: Monthly summaries on TzJ's Net
channel.description: Recent content in Monthly summaries on TzJ's Net

item.title: Monthly Summary 2026-03
item.description: This month’s work was highly focused on the MIHD spatial transcriptomics multimodal clustering research project: after completing environment initialization on the DCC HPC cluster, we systematically integrated the TEDDY-G 400M foundation model, Leiden clustering algorithm, and STHD pathology annotation Ground Truth. Multi-encoder benchmarks revealed STAIG framework’s high robustness to gene encoders and the systematic transfer loss of Foundation Models on clustering tasks. The paper’s Fig 1–6 framework was finalized, marking the project’s transition from the experimental phase to the paper-writing phase.

item.title: Monthly Summary 2026-02
item.description: Intensive research month achieving breakthrough performance improvements in spatial transcriptomics (MIHD) and revealing fundamental limitations in robotic error recovery systems. MIHD project advanced through systematic debugging (coordinate bugs, checkpoint loading, hyperparameter misalignment) and infrastructure optimization (100-500x speedups, embedding caching), achieving valid research-grade metrics (ARI 0.17+). Simultaneously, Error Recovery benchmark scaled from 30 to 2000+ scenes but uncovered catastrophic VLA failure (0% success rates despite fine-tuning), exposing severe distribution shift problems requiring architectural rethinking. Month demonstrated that reproducibility requires holistic alignment across code, hyperparameters, environment, and downstream tools—not just correct logic.
